title: antrophyum cajenense
intro: Antrophyum cajenense, also known as the "tree fern," is a species of fern native to Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Antrophyum cajenense include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Antrophyum cajenense is typically prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, and it can also be used in tincture or capsule form as a herbal supplement.
